title: davallia solida var. arctotheca
intro: Davallia solida var. arctotheca, also known as the leather fern or rabbit's foot fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Davallia genus and is native to South Africa and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Davallia solida var. arctotheca include its ability to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of fever, as well as its use in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Davallia solida var. arctotheca can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often used to treat various health issues.
